title: OOF sequence-model sidecar blended into the tabular tree
summary: Recent pit-stop work found Bi-LSTM strongest among several sequence architectures because pit calls depend on temporal trends, not just current state.
rationale: Recent pit-stop work found Bi-LSTM strongest among several sequence architectures because pit calls depend on temporal trends, not just current state. Your best solutions already exploit hand-built trend proxies, so the next step is to let a small sequential model summarize the last 8-12 laps and hand its OOF score or embedding to the stronger tabular model.
implementation_hint: Train a compact GRU/BiLSTM/TCN on ordered lap windows per `(Year, Race, Driver)` with causal inputs only. Export out-of-fold next-lap probabilities and optionally a 4-16D penultimate-layer embedding, then blend or stack with CatBoost/LightGBM using the current handcrafted features.
expected_effect: Most likely to add orthogonal signal on pace trend, stint evolution, and post-pit recovery patterns that trees approximate only crudely.
risk: Random row CV will massively overstate gains. Sequence windows and OOF generation must respect race order and never mix future laps from the same driver-race into training.
